claims.claim: The token-count compression from BPE (down to 0.51 tokens/byte at 256 merges) reliably shortens sequences, but the resulting vocabulary expansion and context sparsity negate the advantage once the model has sufficient local context.

claims.claim: Given a token stream produced by the tokenizer, the model estimates conditional probabilities: $$P(t_i \mid t_{i-n+1}, \ldots, t_{i-1}) = \frac{c(t_{i-n+1}, \ldots, t_i) + \alpha}{c(t_{i-n+1}, \ldots, t_{i-1}) + \alpha \cdot |V|}$$ where $|V|$ is the vocabulary size (256 + number of merges) and $\alpha$ is a fixed smoothing parameter.
